// These tests will validate the group stage is rewritten when the '_id' field has a $dateTrunc
-    // expression. Some rewrites can only be performed if we are on mongod, since the
-    // `requiresTimeseriesExtendedRangeSupport` flag may not be accurate on mongos.
-    // TODO (SERVER-94666) revisit conditions in which query-level rewrites can be applied, specifically
// Validate the rewrite occurs with a simple case, where the bucket boundary and 'unit' are the
// Validate the rewrite occurs with a timezone with the same hourly boundaries, and
// The 'unit' field in $dateTrunc is larger than 'week', but 'bucketMaxSpanSeconds' is less than
+    // Validate the rewrite does not apply when the collection has extended-range measurements
+    // (timestamps outside [1970-01-01, 2038-01-19]), even though the $dateTrunc expression aligns
+    // with the bucket boundaries. Extended-range buckets can be clamped so that 'control.min' does
+    // not equal the true rounded minimum, so bucket-level fields can't safely stand in for the
*     # Explain of a resolved view must be executed by mongos.
*     # Refusing to run a test that issues an aggregation command with explain because it may
+ *     # checkExplain() asserts there is exactly one $_internalUnpackBucket stage, but
+ *     # getAggPlanStages() returns one match per shard for a sharded explain. If the balancer
+ *     # moves this collection's chunks across shards mid-test, the assertion can see more than
+ *     # one stage even though each shard's plan is individually correct.
+// Verify event filter is absent for an aligned $lt predicate on a fixed-bucket collection.
+// This exercises the shard-side prune pass: after the router processes the pipeline with
+// fixedBuckets=false (setting an eventFilter), the shard sets fixedBuckets=true and should
+    // Set bucketMaxSpanSeconds == bucketRoundingSeconds to satisfy canUseFixedBucketOptimizations.
+    // Insert documents spanning two bucket boundaries (each bucket is 1 hour).
+    // Predicate aligned to the hour boundary: $lt 09:00 covers the [08:00, 09:00) bucket exactly.
+    // Verify no eventFilter or wholeBucketFilter in the explain (null = absent).
+    // Verify correct results: only x:1 and x:2 are before 09:00.
*  # TODO SERVER-76583: Remove following two tags.
+    // Confirms the fixed-bucket write-path optimization is disabled for the whole collection once
+    // any extended-range measurement is present, even though the target bucket for this update is
+    // itself a normal, non-extended-range bucket. Omits the '_id' bound used in the equivalent
+    // aligned-predicate tests above, since that bound is unsafe once extended-range data is
+    // present (an ObjectId's embedded timestamp can't represent dates outside the standard range).
+                // The optimization must not drop this: it stays equal to the original predicate.
* The following tests confirm that the predicates generated from the fixed bucket rewrites
* return the expected results for delete commands.
+    // Confirms the fixed-bucket write-path optimization is not applied when the collection
+    // contains extended-range data (timestamps outside the standard [1970-01-01, 2038-01-19]
+    // range), mirroring the read-path coverage in bucket_unpacking_with_match_fixed_buckets.js
+    // return false for the whole collection once any extended-range measurement is present, even
+    // though the target bucket for this delete is otherwise a normal, non-extended-range bucket.
+    // Uses the same '$gte times[3]' predicate as testFindOneAndRemove_NoFilter above, which is
+    // bucket-boundary-aligned and would have its residualFilter dropped if fixedBuckets were
+    // (incorrectly) applied. As with the update case above, there's no '_id' bound either, since
+    // that predicate is unsafe once extended-range data is present.
+                // The optimization must not drop this: it stays equal to the original predicate.
+// Raw bucket inserts deliberately construct misaligned buckets to be caught by validate() later.
+// timeseriesLessStrictBucketValidator relaxes the write-path document validator (which otherwise
+// enforces the same fixed-bucketing rounding invariant) so those inserts succeed; it does not
// TODO(SERVER-127534): Remove once featureFlagFixedBucketingCatalog is on by default.
+// Verifies fixed-bucket optimizations are gated per-shard, not by the router: for the same
+// aligned, otherwise-prunable predicate, a shard with extended-range data must keep its event
+// filter and skip the _id predicate optimization, while a shard without it gets both.
+        // Fixed bucketing relies on catalog state that only exists for viewless timeseries
+        // collections. In suites that may still create legacy "viewful" collections (e.g.
+        // viewful collections are still the default), this test isn't guaranteed to observe
+    // Split into two chunks at a boundary between the meta value used for extended-range
+    // (pre-1970) data and the one used for standard-range data.
+    // Insert before moving chunks so both inserts land deterministically on the primary shard;
+    // the subsequent moveChunk then migrates the upper chunk's data along with it.
+            // Extended-range data; stays on the primary shard (shard0).
+            // Standard-range data; its chunk moves to shard1 below.
+    // Move the upper chunk (and its data) off of the primary shard.
+    // Aligned to the hour bucket boundary, so the event filter is prunable wherever there's no
+        // shard0 has extended-range data, so its event filter must not be pruned and it must not
+        // get an _id predicate; the other shard has none, so both optimizations should apply.
+    // Shard-side event filter pruning for fixed buckets.
+    // A pipeline that arrived from the router with fixedBuckets=false will have its post-unpack
+    // $match already processed: a loose $match inserted before us, and _eventFilter set inside us.
+    // After populateUnpackBucketStagesFromCollection sets fixedBuckets=true, we check here whether
+    // the event filter is made redundant by the fixed-bucket alignment guarantee. Safety checks:
+    //   3. A $match stage immediately precedes us (belt-and-suspenders: confirms the bucket-level
+    //      filter actually exists before we drop per-event evaluation).
+    //      (the predicate is aligned to bucket boundaries, so the bucket filter is tight).
+            // The router (which didn't yet know fixedBuckets=true) may have already computed a
+            // wholeBucketFilter alongside the now-redundant event filter. As elsewhere in this
+            // file: if the event filter is dropped, a wholeBucketFilter is no longer needed.
+    // Simulate a pipeline that arrived on a shard from the router.
+    // The router had fixedBuckets=false, so it produced:
+    // doOptimize should remove the redundant eventFilter.
+    // hour-aligned date: 2012-10-26T09:00:00Z is a bucket boundary for 3600s buckets.
+    // The loose bucket match the router generated before the unpack stage.
+    auto looseBucketMatch = BSON("$match" << BSON("control.max.foo" << BSON("$gte" << date)));
+    // The unpack stage as serialized by the router: fixedBuckets=false, eventFilter present.
+    // Simulate populateUnpackBucketStagesFromCollection setting fixedBuckets=true on the shard.
+    auto unpackIt = std::next(sources.begin());  // second stage is the unpack
+    // Shard runs doOptimize. Expect the eventFilter to be pruned.
+        << "eventFilter should be pruned when fixedBuckets=true and predicate is aligned; got: "
+    // Simulate a pipeline that arrived on a shard from the router with fixedBuckets=false.
+    // Since the router didn't yet know the predicate would turn out to be an exact match, its
+    // $match-processing pass (the one at the "Attempt to map predicates on bucketed fields"
+    // block) computed both a wholeBucketFilter and an eventFilter for the non-fixed-bucket case.
+    // Once the shard learns fixedBuckets=true and determines the predicate is exact, both the
+    // eventFilter and the now-redundant wholeBucketFilter must be pruned.
+    // hour-aligned date: 2012-10-26T09:00:00Z is a bucket boundary for 3600s buckets.
+    auto looseBucketMatch = BSON("$match" << BSON("control.max.foo" << BSON("$gte" << date)));
+    // The unpack stage as serialized by the router: fixedBuckets=false, both filters present.
+    // Simulate populateUnpackBucketStagesFromCollection setting fixedBuckets=true on the shard.
+        << "eventFilter should be pruned when fixedBuckets=true and predicate is aligned; got: "
+        << "wholeBucketFilter is redundant once the eventFilter is pruned; got: "
+    auto looseBucketMatch = BSON("$match" << BSON("control.max.foo" << BSON("$gte" << date)));
+    // 'usesExtendedRange' is carried on the '$_internalUnpackBucket' BSON spec itself (it round-
+    // trips into '_sharedState->_bucketUnpacker', which is the source of truth doOptimize checks),
+    // rather than being set via a separate stage-level setter.
+                               << "usesExtendedRange" << true  // shard has extended range data
// to the buckets before unpacking. So we can use default values
// for the rest of the arguments.

-        // predicate generator selection below ignores extended-range data on the write path. It
